header.name: Deva Anand
header.tagline: I design systems that make ML actually scale.

about.title: about me
paragraphs: I'm an MS CS student at UMass Amherst. I spent a lot of time in ML Systems and Backend Engineering, obsessing over how to make models run faster and cheaper.

But lately, I've have been more intrested AI Safety, Mechanistic Interpretability, and Causal Reasoning. I want to understand how these black boxes actually "think"—specifically looking at internal circuits for planning and verifying if agents are using valid logic versus just memorizing correlations.
I'm always open to chatting about Safety, Interpretability, or building reliable guardrails. Hit me up at: devaanand@umass.edu

entries.description: Pursuing my Master of Science in Computer Science at the University of Massachusetts Amherst, trying to turn my obsession with ML systems into useful research and real-world impact.

Backend Engineer at Wysa. Worked on scalable mental health tooling, including customizable NHS eTriage systems integrated with Mayden’s iaptus platform, contributing to revenue and impact at clinical scale.

Built a full-stack annotation tool from scratch for the AI team, replacing manual sheet-based workflows and saving over 100 hours of manual work per month. Also fixed 20+ security vulnerabilities from VAPT reports, hardening core systems and reducing potential attack surface.

Open Source Contributor & Freelancer. Developed a full-featured gym website and management system using the MERN stack, with authentication, membership management, and payment flows that streamlined day-to-day operations.

Automated payment-triggered email notifications and periodic background jobs, cutting the client’s manual workload significantly. Contributed to multiple open-source projects (like LinkFree, FreeHit, and ProjectsHut), adding new features and improving both backend and frontend codebases.

Backend Developer Intern at Cario Growth Services Pvt. Ltd. Built and maintained over 20 API endpoints in Node.js and Fastify, with solid database integrations for the core product.

Integrated LLMs to power an auto-commenting feature, improving the contextual quality of responses in chat. Helped migrate core services to Fastify and gradually move codebases from JavaScript to TypeScript, improving maintainability and type safety.

B.E. in Computer Science and Engineering at Vels Institute of Science, Technology and Advanced Studies, Chennai. This is where I properly fell in love with CS from ML and AI to Distributed and Cloud Computing and decided to double down on systems and ML as my long-term path.

writing.title: writing
writing.subtitle: I occasionally write on medium and X

articles.title: Measuring Memory Access Patterns (Sequential vs Strided vs Random)
The "Middleman" Trap: A Better Way to Handle File Uploads
why doesn't the database just auto-create all the indexes for me
Northern lights: how do they form?

items.name: AI Research Scout
items.stack: Cloudflare Workers · Hono · Workers AI · D1 · React 19
items.description: is a RAG-based research assistant on Cloudflare: paste URLs, it fetches each page, strips HTML, chunks and embeds with bge-base-en-v1.5, stores everything in D1, and answers your questions via cosine similarity retrieval — using only what it actually read. Chat streams over WebSockets via AIChatAgent with history persisted in Durable Object storage.

items.name: Refusal Decay
items.stack: Python · PyTorch · Hugging Face · Llama-3.2-3B · SLURM
items.description: is a mechanistic interpretability study tracing how the refusal direction in a safety-aligned LLM’s residual stream decays under prefilling attacks — token by token, layer by layer — to determine whether early forced compliance causally erodes the model’s ability to refuse. Implements difference-in-means probing, per-token projection tracing, and direction-patching experiments on Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct.

items.name: AgenticSearch
items.stack: Python · FastAPI · OpenAI · Brave Search · SQLite
items.description: is a provenance-first entity discovery system: takes a free-text topic query and returns a ranked table of real-world entities with per-cell evidence — source URL, snippet, and confidence score — across a 10-stage pipeline covering schema planning, scraping, reranking, deterministic extraction, deduplication, and verification.

items.name: TasteGraph
items.description: is a production-grade cross-media identity mapping MVP where users log films, books, music, food, places, and ideas to generate a taste fingerprint and discover people through overlap stories and rare shared tastes.

items.name: Knowledge-Graph-Guided RAG
items.description: is a team-built KG-guided RAG pipeline for multi-hop QA on HotpotQA, decomposing complex questions into single-hop sub-questions, fusing multi-view FAISS evidence with RRF, cross-encoder reranking, and MMR, then selecting evidence with token-budgeted 0–1 knapsack and knowledge-graph reasoning to improve supporting-fact recall over the KG2RAG baseline.

items.name: Spark ETL Optimization
items.description: is a performance deep-dive on the NYC TLC taxi dataset, where I start from a naive Apache Spark ETL pipeline and iteratively optimize partitioning, column pruning, precomputed fields, and skew handling to reduce runtime, tail latency, and resource usage.

items.name: Spend - AI Expense Tracker
items.description: is a zero-friction personal finance tool that uses AI to parse natural language inputs (e.g., ‘chipotle $14’) into structured expenses, featuring income tracking, monthly budgets, and recurring bills.

items.name: ReferralFinder
items.description: is a production-ready SaaS platform that helps students, especially international students in the US, prioritize jobs, estimate sponsorship likelihood, tailor ATS-friendly applications, and generate referral outreach workflows without scraping or automating LinkedIn.

items.name: Team Sonare — Sign ↔ Speech (Offline)
items.description: is a fully offline, privacy-preserving Sign ↔ Speech translator built for the Qualcomm Hackathon 2025 with Team Sonare, using an Electron + React UI, MediaPipe gesture tracking, a FastAPI backend, and whisper.cpp to run the entire pipeline locally on edge devices.

items.name: Underdogs Fitness Platform
items.description: is a full-stack gym management system built as a freelance project, with separate backend and frontend repos, handling member onboarding, plans, payments, and day-to-day operations using a clean API-first architecture.

items.name: Alzheimer’s Disease Classification
items.description: is a deep learning project for classifying Alzheimer’s disease from medical imaging, exploring different architectures, training setups, and evaluation metrics as part of my undergrad capstone project, which was later published in an IEEE conference.

items.name: Goalsetter App
items.description: is a MERN + Redux app for setting, tracking, and managing personal goals, featuring authentication, CRUD workflows, and a simple dashboard to keep long-term goals organized.

items.name: Tinder Clone
items.description: is a Tinder-inspired MERN stack clone that experiments with swipe-style interactions, matchmaking logic, and real-time updates using a modern React + Node.js setup.

items.title: Deep Learning-Based Alzheimer's Disease Classification using Transfer Learning and Data Augmentation
items.authors: Deva Anand; P. Sheela Gowr; S. Logesh; S. M. Anand

items.description: Started The Inheritance Games, first real dive into fiction, let's see how that goes.
Posted my first reel after moving to the US, first snowfall at UMass Amherst and it somehow hit ~7k views.
Saw first northern lights of my life in Amherst, MA
Participated in Qualcomm Edge AI hackathon at NYU in NYC and built an edge AI project and presented
Moved to United States of America 🇺🇸 for my MS in CS at UMass Amherst. Excited for the journey ahead!
